title: Automate repetitive Case processing flows
aiRationale: 4 active low-complexity AutoLaunchedFlow(s) are processing 300 Case records in 90 days (activity score 2.13, threshold 0.6). These flows perform high-volume repetitive logic with fewer than 5 elements each — a strong candidate for an Agentforce agent that handles decisioning and exception routing rather than a static flow.

title: Reduce case routing friction with intelligent assignment
aiRationale: 480 owner changes recorded across 300 Cases in 90 days — an average of 1.6 reassignments per case (threshold 1.5). Agents are manually re-routing cases that pattern analysis could route correctly on first assignment. An intelligent routing agent would reduce time-to-resolution and eliminate repetitive escalation cycles.

title: Automate knowledge article surfacing at case resolution
aiRationale: 60 Cases closed in 90 days — only 30 had a linked Knowledge Article (knowledge gap score 0.50, threshold 0.4). Resolution agents are not consistently reusing KB content, increasing handle time and reducing deflection. An Agentforce agent could surface relevant KB articles during resolution and auto-link them on close.

title: Streamline Discount Approval approval bottleneck
aiRationale: 60 records are pending in the 'Discount Approval' approval process with an average delay of 5.0 days (threshold 3.0 days). 2 approver(s) are handling all pending items (bottleneck score 30.0). An automation agent could pre-validate submissions, surface approval context, and escalate stale approvals automatically.

title: Redistribute approval workload for 'Discount Approval'
aiRationale: 60 records are queued in 'Discount Approval' with only 2 approver(s) — a bottleneck score of 30.0 pending items per approver (threshold 10.0). This approval concentration creates a single point of failure. An automation layer could distribute assignments intelligently or escalate to backup approvers when the queue exceeds acceptable depth.

title: Consolidate flows referencing 'ServiceNow Integration'
aiRationale: 3 active Flow(s) independently call the 'ServiceNow Integration' Named Credential (threshold 3 distinct flows). These flows contain duplicated integration callout logic with no shared error handling or governance layer. An agent-based orchestration pattern would centralise the integration, add retry/fallback handling, and reduce maintenance risk.

title: Automate cross-system record sync (Salesforce ↔ external)
aiRationale: Cross-system echo detected: 75 Salesforce Cases reference external ticket IDs (SF echo score 0.25), 80 ServiceNow incidents reference SF cases (SN score 0.16), 62 Jira issues reference SF cases (Jira score 0.22). Agents are manually duplicating the same issue across systems — a pattern that agent-based bidirectional sync would eliminate, reducing manual effort and improving resolution continuity across teams.
